D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Transition Metals — d-d Splitting Sets the Colour

an octahedral ligand field splits the five d orbitals into t₂g and eg; the gap Δ₀ is the photon energy of the d→d absorption — and the colour you see is its complement

A transition-metal cation in an octahedral field of six ligands no longer has five degenerate d orbitals. The three pointing between ligands (t₂g) drop in energy; the two pointing at ligands (eg) rise. The energy gap Δ₀ between them sets a single absorption band. The light we see is everything that wasn't absorbed — the complementary colour.

Drag Δ₀. The octahedron shows M and six ligands; the energy-level diagram beside it shows t₂g (lower) and eg (upper) with the gap; the absorption band on the spectrum follows λ = hc/Δ₀; the swatch on the right shows the perceived complement.
